title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen and Professor Yardley discuss the withdrawal of the police presence from the investigation, which Yardley notes was ordered by the county sheriff. Ellery suggests that the police focus is too narrow, targeting only Stephen Megalos and Andrew Van (the Tewell brothers). He observes that Megalos is protected by his squad and access to waterways, while Van utilizes disguises. Ellery alludes to a meaningful connection involving a chess game story that Yardley has failed to grasp, before leaving the professor to take a walk.

While walking through the dark countryside toward the Bradwood area, Ellery passes two detectives on a porch. As he approaches a path leading to a gazebo and a totem pole, he overhears a tense argument between Jonathan Lincoln and Helen Brad near a tennis court. Helen accuses Jonathan of cowardice regarding an encounter with a man named Roman. Feeling unappreciated for his work for men who are either at sea or playing chess, Jonathan declares his intention to leave his employment and go with Hester, noting he informed Melchior of this decision on a yacht earlier that day.

The argument intensifies when Helen reveals that Stephen Megalos's year-long trip was actually motivated by his desire to marry her. Two days later, Ellery tells Professor Yardley that justice is merely "taking a break," observing that Officer Vane is acting belligerently due to a lack of concrete evidence. Yardley suggests that Ellery should forget the case and relax.
